scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that on 10.01.2024, she entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant regarding the purchase of a residential property being Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. A copy of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A.

6. The Plaintiff states that pursuant to the terms of the said Agreement, she paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution of the sale deed. A copy of the Bank Receipt is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B.

7. The Plaintiff states that the schedule property is more fully described hereinafter and is identified by Survey No. {{PROPERTY_SURVEY_NUMBER}} situated at Koramangala, Bengaluru. The Plaintiff states that the property is free from all encumbrances as represented by the Defendant at the time of the Agreement.

8. The Plaintiff states that she has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract in accordance with the terms thereof. {{READINESS_DETAILS}} The Plaintiff states that she maintained the requisite funds for the balance payment and sought execution of the sale deed within the stipulated time.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

9. The Plaintiff states that despite her readiness and willingness, the Defendant vide letter dated 05.06.2024 refused to execute the sale deed, claiming that he had received a higher offer for the said property. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024 is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure D.

10. The Plaintiff states that she subsequently issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed in terms of the Agreement. The Defendant received the said notice but failed to comply with the requisition. A copy of the Legal Notice with postal receipt is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C.

11. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant has committed a clear breach of the contract dated 10.01.2024 by refusing to transfer the property despite receipt of the advance payment and the legal notice. The Defendant's actions have caused wrongful loss to the Plaintiff and wrongful gain to the Defendant.

DAMAGES

12. The Plaintiff states that by reason of the Defendant's breach, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the benefit of the property which has appreciated in value since the date of the Agreement. The Plaintiff has been kept out of the advance payment of Rs. 25,00,000/- which was intended for the acquisition of the said immovable property.

13. The Plaintiff states that she has incurred costs and expenses in issuing the legal notice and instituting the present suit. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the costs of the suit and such further compensation as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit to award in the interest of justice.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The present cause is governed by Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment — court SHALL grant unless S.11(2), S.14, or S.16 exception), Section 16, Section 21, and Section 22.

15. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

16. The Plaintiff also relies on Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, directing the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) In the alternative, if specific performance is not granted, pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages;
    (c) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 12 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any othe

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A Company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when contract is broken; or for continuing breach, when it ceases. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff states that the mandatory pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 has been {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATUS}}. {{MEDIATION_CERTIFICATE_DETAILS}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a Software Development Agreement for the development of an ERP system, wherein the total contract value was fixed at Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant as the first milestone payment on 01.02.2024 via NEFT bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is marked as **Annexure B**.

10. As per the agreed timeline under the Agreement, the Defendant was contractually obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024.

BREACH PARTICULARS

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the stipulated deadline of 01.05.2024, thereby committing a breach of the terms specified under {{BREACHED_CLAUSE_NUMBERS}} of the Agreement.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project entirely without delivering any module and failed to refund the advance payment received. Email correspondence evidencing the abandonment of the project by the Defendant is marked as **Annexure C**.

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the amount and compensate for losses. A copy of the legal notice is marked as **Annexure E**. The Defendant replied to the said notice stating {{DEFENDANT_NOTICE_RESPONSE}}.

DAMAGES

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant, which constitutes the actual loss suffered due to the breach.

15. Due to the non-delivery and delay in operations, the Plaintiff suffered consequential damages amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/-. The assessment of such loss is based on {{CALCULATION_METHOD_CONSEQUENTIAL}} as detailed in the loss assessment report by the auditor, marked as **Annexure D**.

16. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the total claimed amount from the date of breach until realisation.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

18. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

19. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

20. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

21. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

23. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages;
    (b) Award Rs. {{ACTUAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/- towards Actual Loss;
    (c) Award Rs. {{CONSEQUENTIAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/- towards Consequential loss / damages;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (e) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

24. Actual Loss
   Amount claimed: Rs. {{ACTUAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/-
   Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
   Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

25. Consequential loss / damages
   Amount claimed: Rs. {{CONSEQUENTIAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/-
   Basis / methodology: {{CONSEQUENTIAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
   Documentary proof: {{CONSEQUENTIAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) for Rs.20,00,000/-

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment of project

Ann
